title: The case for fixing everything
optimized_headline: Why Repairing Everything Could Transform Our Lives in Unexpected Ways
raw_excerpt: The handsome new book Maintenance: Of Everything, Part One, by the tech industry legend Stewart Brand, promises to be the first in a series offering “a comprehensive overview of the civilizational importance of maintenance.” One of Brand’s several biographers described him as a mainstay of both counterculture and cyberculture, and with Maintenance, Brand wants us…
raw_body: The handsome new book Maintenance: Of Everything, Part One, by the tech industry legend Stewart Brand, promises to be the first in a series offering “a comprehensive overview of the civilizational importance of maintenance.” One of Brand’s several biographers described him as a mainstay of both counterculture and cyberculture, and with Maintenance, Brand wants us…
speedrun: Stewart Brand's new book, Maintenance: Of Everything, Part One, emphasizes the critical role of maintenance in our civilization. It aims to shift our perspective on how we view and treat our infrastructure and technology. By highlighting the often-overlooked importance of upkeep, Brand argues that neglecting maintenance can lead to broader societal issues. This conversation is timely as many systems are showing signs of wear and tear in today's rapidly changing world.
why_it_matters: This book could impact policymakers and city planners by encouraging them to prioritize maintenance in budget allocations.
It reflects a broader trend where society is starting to recognize the value of sustainability and longevity over constant innovation.

lenses.eli12: Stewart Brand's book talks about why taking care of our things is super important. Think of maintenance like watering a plant; if you don't, it wilts. By focusing on upkeep, we can keep our communities and technologies running smoothly for longer. This matters because it affects how we live and interact with our environment every day.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, Brand's insights highlight the need to integrate maintenance into product lifecycle planning. Understanding user needs for ongoing support could enhance customer satisfaction and reduce long-term costs. This approach might encourage businesses to develop more durable products that require less frequent replacement.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, Brand's emphasis on maintenance can lead to better design practices that prioritize longevity. Engineers might consider how regular upkeep can extend the life of systems and reduce failure rates. This aligns with the growing trend of designing for sustainability, which could influence future engineering standards.
